title: Access Control Checks in Spring Applications
description: Ensure access control decisions are based on strong checks in Spring applications to prevent security vulnerabilities.
recommendation: Implement robust access control checks in Spring applications.
impact: Prevent security vulnerabilities and potential misuse of critical functionalities.

checks.message: Access control using @PreAuthorize, @PostAuthorize, or @Secured should not rely on weak conditions.
Access control should not permit all users or only check for authenticated status without role verification.
Public methods should have explicit access control checks.
Methods returning collections should use @PreFilter or @PostFilter to ensure fine-grained access control.
